description: Table monstats2 du mod TCP (colonnes documentees via le D2R Data Guide).
source: Colonnes du .txt (mod TCP) ; types/descriptions du D2R Data Guide (guide/).

columns.name: Id
columns.description: Controls the unique name ID to define the monster. This must match the same value in the monstats.txt file

columns.name: Height
columns.description: Determines the height of the monster. This has 2 purposes. The first purpose is to act as an index value for selecting which icebreak missile to use when the monster dies while frozen. The second purpose is to select a code which affects what attack animation the player characters will use when attacking the monster (Attack1 and or Attack2). See each code description types below.

columns.name: OverlayHeight
columns.description: Determines the height value of overlays (Overlay.txt) for the monster

columns.name: pixHeight
columns.description: Determines the pixel height value for the damage bar when the monster is selected

columns.name: spawnCol
columns.description: Controls the method for spawning the monster based on the collisions in the environment

columns.name: MeleeRng
columns.description: Controls the range of the monster's melee attack, which can affect also affect certain AI pathing. If this value equals 255, then refer to the monster's weapon class ("BaseW")

columns.name: BaseW
columns.description: Defines the monster's base weapon class, which can affect how the monster attacks. Referenced by the

columns.name: HitClass
columns.description: Defines the specific class of an attack when the monster successfully hits with an attack. This can affect the sound and overlay display of the attack hit. Referenced from the

columns.name: HDv
columns.description: Head visual

columns.name: TRv
columns.description: Torso visual

columns.name: LGv
columns.description: Legs visual

columns.name: Rav
columns.description: Right Arm visual

columns.name: Lav
columns.description: Left Arm visual

columns.name: RHv
columns.description: Right Hand visual

columns.name: LHv
columns.description: Left Hand visual

columns.name: SHv
columns.description: Shield visual

columns.name: HD
columns.description: Head

columns.name: TR
columns.description: Torso

columns.name: LG
columns.description: Legs

columns.name: RA
columns.description: Right Arm

columns.name: LA
columns.description: Left Arm

columns.name: RH
columns.description: Right Hand

columns.name: LH
columns.description: Left Hand

columns.name: SH
columns.description: Shield

columns.name: TotalPieces
columns.description: Defines the total amount of component pieces that the monster uses. This value should match the number of enabled Boolean fields listed above

columns.name: mDT
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the Death Mode for the monster. If equals 0, then this mode is disabled

columns.name: mNU
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the Neutral Mode for the monster. If equals 0, then this mode is disabled

columns.name: mWL
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the Walk Mode for the monster. If equals 0, then this mode is disabled

columns.name: mGH
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the Get Hit Mode for the monster. If equals 0, then this mode is disabled

columns.name: mBL
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the Block Mode for the monster. If equals 0, then this mode is disabled

columns.name: mSC
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the Cast Mode for the monster. If equals 0, then this mode is disabled

columns.name: mDD
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the Dead Mode for the monster. If equals 0, then this mode is disabled

columns.name: mKB
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the Knockback Mode for the monster. If equals 0, then this mode is disabled

columns.name: mSQ
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the Sequence Mode for the monster. If equals 0, then this mode is disabled

columns.name: mRN
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the Run Mode for the monster. If equals 0, then this mode is disabled

columns.name: dDT
columns.description: Defines the number of directions that the monster can face during Death Mode

columns.name: dNU
columns.description: Defines the number of directions that the monster can face during Neutral Mode

columns.name: dWL
columns.description: Defines the number of directions that the monster can face during Walk Mode

columns.name: dGH
columns.description: Defines the number of directions that the monster can face during Get Hit Mode

columns.name: dBL
columns.description: Defines the number of directions that the monster can face during Block Mode

columns.name: dSC
columns.description: Defines the number of directions that the monster can face during Cast Mode

columns.name: dDD
columns.description: Defines the number of directions that the monster can face during Dead Mode

columns.name: dKB
columns.description: Defines the number of directions that the monster can face during Knockback Mode

columns.name: dSQ
columns.description: Defines the number of directions that the monster can face during Sequence Mode

columns.name: dRN
columns.description: Defines the number of directions that the monster can face during Run Mode

columns.name: SCmv
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the Cast Mode while the monster is moving with the Walk mode or Run mode. If equals 0, then this mode is disabled while the monster is moving

columns.name: noGfxHitTest
columns.description: If equals 1, then enable the mouse selection bounding box functionality around the monster. If equals 0, then the monster cannot be selected by the mouse

columns.name: htTop
columns.description: Define the pixel top offset around the monster for the mouse selection bounding box functionality. This field relies on the

columns.name: htLeft
columns.description: Define the pixel left offset around the monster for the mouse selection bounding box functionality. This field relies on the

columns.name: htWidth
columns.description: Define the pixel right offset around the monster for the mouse selection bounding box functionality. This field relies on the

columns.name: htHeight
columns.description: Define the pixel bottom offset around the monster for the mouse selection bounding box functionality. This field relies on the

columns.name: restore
columns.description: Determines if the monster should be placed on the inactive list, to be saved when the level unloads. If equals 0, then do not save the monster. If equals 1, then rely on other checks to determine to save the monster. If equals 2, then force save the monster

columns.name: automapCel
columns.description: Controls what index of the Automap tiles to use to display this monster on the Automap. This field relies on the

columns.name: noMap
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster will not appear on the Automap. If equals 0, then the monster will normally appear on the Automap

columns.name: noOvly
columns.description: If equals 1, then no looping overlays will be drawn on the monster. If equals 0, then overlays will be drawn on the monster. (

columns.name: isSel
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster is selectable and can be targeted. If equals 0, then the monster cannot be selected

columns.name: alSel
columns.description: If equals 1, then the player can always select the monster, regardless of being an ally or enemy. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: noSel
columns.description: If equals 1, then the player can never select the monster. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: shiftSel
columns.description: If equals 1, then the player can target this monster when holding the Shift key and clicking to use a skill. If equals 0, then the monster cannot be targeted while the player is holding the Shift key

columns.name: corpseSel
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster's corpse can be with the mouse cursor. If equals 0, then the monster's corpse cannot be selected with the mouse cursor

columns.name: isAtt
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster can be attacked. If equals 0, then the monster cannot be attacked

columns.name: revive
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster is allowed to be revived by the Necromancer Revive skill. If equals 0, then the monster cannot be revived by the Necromancer Revive skill

columns.name: limitCorpses
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster's corpse will be placed into a pool with all other corpses with this field checked. Once that pool reaches the max (50), the corpses at the beginning of the pool start getting removed

columns.name: critter
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster will be flagged as a critter, which gives some special case handling such as not creating impact sounds and differently handling its spawn placement in presets. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: small
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster will be classified as a small type, which can affect what types of missiles can be used on the monster (Example: Barbarian Grim Ward size) or how the monster is knocked back. If equals 0, then ignore this. If this field is enabled, then the

columns.name: large
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster will be classified as a large type, which can affect what types of missiles can be used on the monster (Example: Barbarian Grim Ward size) or how the monster is knocked back. If equals 0, then ignore this. If this field is enabled, then the

columns.name: soft
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster's corpse is classified as soft bodied, meaning that its corpse can be used by certain corpse skills such as Barbarian Find Potion, Find Item, or Grim Ward. If equals 0, then the monster's corpse cannot be used for these skills

columns.name: inert
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster will never attack its enemies. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: objCol
columns.description: If equals 1 and the monster class is "barricadedoor", "barricadedoor2", or "evilhut", then the monster will place an invisible object with collision. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: deadCol
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster's corpse will have collision with other units. If equals 0, then the monster's corpse will not have collision

columns.name: unflatDead
columns.description: If equals 1, then ignore the corpse draw order for rendering the Sprite on top of others, while the monster is dead. If equals 0, then the monster's corpse will have a normal corpse draw order

columns.name: Shadow
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster will project a shadow on the ground. If equals 0, then the monster will not project a shadow

columns.name: noUniqueShift
columns.description: If equals 1 and the monster is a Unique monster, then the monster will not have random color palette transform shifts. If equals 0, then the non-Unique monster will have random color palette transform shifts

columns.name: compositeDeath
columns.description: If equals 1, then the monster's Death Mode and Dead mode will make use of its component system. If equals 0, then the monster will default to using the Hand-To-Hand weapon class and no component system

columns.name: localBlood
columns.description: Controls the color of the monster's blood based on the region locale. If equals 0, then do not change the blood to green and keep it red. If equals 1, then change the monster's special components to use the green blood locale. If equals 2, then change the blood to green

columns.name: Bleed
columns.description: Controls if the monster will create blood missiles. If equals 0, then the monster will never bleed. If equals 1, then the monster will randomly create the "blood1" or "blood2" missiles when hit. If equals 2, then the monster will randomly create the "blood1", "blood2", "bigblood1", or "bigblood2" missiles when hit

columns.name: Light
columns.description: Controls the monster's minimum Light Radius size (measured in grid sub-tiles)

columns.name: light-r
columns.description: Controls the red color value of the monster's Light Radius (Uses a value from 0 to 255)

columns.name: light-g
columns.description: Controls the green color value of the monster's Light Radius (Uses a value from 0 to 255)

columns.name: light-b
columns.description: Controls the blue color value of the monster's Light Radius (Uses a value from 0 to 255)

columns.name: InfernoLen
columns.description: The frame length to hold the channel cast time of the inferno skill. This is used for when the monster has the "inferno" state, or for Diablo when he is using the "DiabLight" skill

columns.name: InfernoAnim
columns.description: The exact frame in the channel animation to loop back and start at again

columns.name: InfernoRollback
columns.description: The exact frame in the channel animation to determine when to roll back to the

columns.name: ResurrectMode
columns.description: Controls which monster mode to set on the monster when it is resurrected. Referenced from the

columns.name: ResurrectSkill
columns.description: Controls what skill should the monster use when it is resurrected (

columns.name: SpawnUniqueMod
columns.description: Controls what unique modifier the monster should always spawn with (
